content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 8,
1992
Mr.
Gregory McConnell
The McConnell Group
Rental Housing Consultants
2041 Bancroft Way,
Suite 201
Berkeley,
CA
94704
RE:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92-612
Dear Mr.
McConnell:
You have asked for advice concerning the lobbying provisions
of the Political Reform Act
(“the Act”)./
QUESTION
What type of filer is the McConnell Group,
a sole
proprietorship that will attempt to influence legislative action?
CONCLUSION
The McConnell Group should file as a lobbyist employer.
FACTS
You are the sole proprietor and receive income from the
McConnell Group,
a rent control consulting firm.
One of your
existing clients is the Black Property Owners Association.
The
Mcconnell Group sponsored legislation
(AB 3350)
during the 1991-92
Legislative Session to benefit rental property owners that own
property in certain rent controlled cities in California.
You
personally lobbied the Legislature on this bill and were the only
person at the McConnell Group who did so.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660


McConnell,
Gregory
A—92—612
-Page
2
According to our telephone conversation on November 19,
1992,
the Black Property Owners Association supported your lobbying
effort but did not retain or employ your firm to lobby on-its
behalf.
Moreover, the Association did not provide funds to
support the lobbying effort.
However,
you did solicit association
members and non members for donations to help offset lobbying
expenses.
You received a few $500.00 donations but the majority
of money was from single contributors of $25-$50.
The McConnell
Group plans to sponsor a similiar bill in the 1993—94 Legislative
Session.
Based on previous telephone advice from the Commission
you registered as a lobbying coalition in July 1992.
V
ANALYSIS
The Act requires persons that employ a lobbyist to register
and file periodic reports.
A lobbyist employer is defined in the
Act as an entity or organization which:
(a)
Employs one or more lobbyists for economic
consideration, other than reimbursement for reasonable
travel expenses, for the purpose of influencing
legislative or administrative action, or
(b)
Contracts for the services of a lobbying firm
for economic consideration, other than reimbursement for
reasonable travel expense, for the purpose of
influencing legislative or administrative action.
Because the McConnell Group compensates you to influence
legislation on its behalf, the McConnell Group must re9ister as a
lobbyist employer for the 1993-94 Legislative Session./
It
appears from the facts that donors are not employing or
contracting with you or the McConnell Group to influence
legislative action on their own behalf.
Therefore,
such persons
will not qualify as lobbyist employers or members of a lobbying
coalition.
Because you complied with advice provided by the
Commission, it is not necessary for you to change your previous
lobbying disclosure statements.
2/
For the fourth quarter lobbyist employer report
(Form 635),
check the box on the cover page to indicate a lobbyist employer
filing.
It will not be necessary to file the Payments Received by
Lobbying Coalition attachment
(Form 635C).


McConnell,
Gregory
A—9 2—612
Page
3
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916)
322—5662 if you
-
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
